rules.reason: SEC-registered U.S. public filers (non-FPI) are required by Regulation S-X Article 4 (17 CFR Part 210, §210.4-01) to present financial statements in accordance with U.S. GAAP.
Per ASC 105-10-05-1, the FASB Accounting Standards Codification is the source of authoritative GAAP for nongovernmental U.S. entities. Default treatment when no other framework is elected.
EU Regulation 1606/2002 requires listed companies in EU member states to prepare consolidated financial statements in accordance with IFRS (IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements).
U.K.-adopted international accounting standards (substantively IFRS) apply to U.K. listed entities post-Brexit. IAS 1 governs presentation.
IFRS for SMEs Section 1 permits use by entities that (a) do not have public accountability and (b) publish general purpose financial statements. Org-election required to activate.
For MFG and RTL entities filing with the SEC, Regulation S-X Article 4 governs presentation form (financial statements must follow U.S. GAAP).

SEC Form 20-F General Instruction G permits foreign private issuers to file IFRS financial statements without U.S. GAAP reconciliation when an explicit unreserved statement of IFRS compliance is made. Org-election required to claim FPI status.
Fallback: U.S. manufacturing entities default to U.S. GAAP under ASC 105-10-05-1 when no jurisdiction-mandated or elected framework applies.
Fallback: U.S. retail entities default to U.S. GAAP under ASC 105-10-05-1 when no jurisdiction-mandated or elected framework applies.

Canadian publicly accountable enterprises follow IFRS per CPA Canada Handbook Part I; private enterprises may elect ASPE (handled by org-election in future rule). This rule intentionally produces a non-US framework to validate the resolver's cross-jurisdiction handling.
